maml:description.maml:para: Enable one or more privileges for an account

Can be used to enable one of the following privileges for one or more users: 'SeNetworkLogonRight', 'SeRemoteInteractiveLogonRight', 'SeBatchLogonRight', 'SeInteractiveLogonRight', 'SeServiceLogonRight', 'SeDenyNetworkLogonRight', 'SeDenyInteractiveLogonRight', 'SeDenyBatchLogonRight', 'SeDenyServiceLogonRight', 'SeDenyRemoteInteractiveLogonRight', 'SeTcbPrivilege', 'SeMachineAccountPrivilege', 'SeIncreaseQuotaPrivilege', 'SeBackupPrivilege', 'SeChangeNotifyPrivilege', 'SeSystemTimePrivilege', 'SeCreateTokenPrivilege', 'SeCreatePagefilePrivilege', 'SeCreateGlobalPrivilege', 'SeDebugPrivilege', 'SeEnableDelegationPrivilege', 'SeRemoteShutdownPrivilege', 'SeAuditPrivilege', 'SeImpersonatePrivilege', 'SeIncreaseBasePriorityPrivilege', 'SeLoadDriverPrivilege', 'SeLockMemoryPrivilege', 'SeSecurityPrivilege', 'SeSystemEnvironmentPrivilege', 'SeManageVolumePrivilege', 'SeProfileSingleProcessPrivilege', 'SeSystemProfilePrivilege', 'SeUndockPrivilege', 'SeAssignPrimaryTokenPrivilege', 'SeRestorePrivilege', 'SeShutdownPrivilege', 'SeSynchAgentPrivilege', 'SeTakeOwnershipPrivilege'
The list of user names that will receive the privileges
The list of privileges to add
The list of user names that will receive the privileges
The list of privileges to add

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables the Impersonate privilege

maml:description.maml:para: This cmdlet enables standard permissions on a CA Template
This cmdlet enables the Read, Enroll and AutoEnroll permissions on one template for one or more accounts
The name of the template for which permissions will be added.
The list of accounts (users, computers, groups) that will receive Read, Enroll and AutoEnroll permissions
The name of the template for which permissions will be added.
The list of accounts (users, computers, groups) that will receive Read, Enroll and AutoEnroll permissions

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gives members of the group Domain Computers the permission to Enroll and AutoEnroll for the template WebServer1

maml:description.maml:para: Sends entire functions to remote sessions
Sends the entire function definition to a remote session, so that scoping is not an issue any more
One or more remote sessions that will receive the functions
The FunctionInfo of the function to send, e.g. (Get-Command Get-Type)
One or more remote sessions that will receive the functions
The FunctionInfo of the function to send, e.g. (Get-Command Get-Type)

command:example.maml:title: ------------------------ Send function ------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\ $Session = New-PSSession myTargetMachine PS C:\ Add-FunctionToPsSession $session -FunctionInfo (Get-Command Test-IsAdministrator)

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sends the function Test-IsAdministrator to the remote session $session

command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Add-TfsAgentUserCapability -InstanceName AL -CollectionName ALProject1 -PoolName Sharknado -AgentId 1 -Capability @{Cap1 = 'Value1'}

maml:description.maml:para: Get one or more certificates
Gets one or more certificates based on a search string in order to add them e.g. to another machine or store. Supports both Cer as well as PFX. Password is required when using PFX.
The search string to use. For more information, see: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.cryptography.x509certificates.x509certificate2collection.find
Sets which attribute will be searched, e.g. FindBySubjectName
The location to search in. Possible values CERT_SYSTEM_STORE_CURRENT_USER, CERT_SYSTEM_STORE_LOCAL_MACHINE, CERT_SYSTEM_STORE_SERVICES, CERT_SYSTEM_STORE_USERS
The store to look in, e.g. My
The name of the service

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $bytes = Invoke-Command SomeHost { Get-Certificate2 -SearchString japete -FindType FindBySubjectName -Location CERT_SYSTEM_STORE_CURRENT_USER -Store My} | Add-Certificate2

dev:remarks.maml:para: Remotely retrieves the certificate for CN=japete and installs it locally.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets all available build steps

Gets all available build steps in a copy-pasteable format that can be copied to a script. Since there are a mulitude of available steps, you can filter on their names, apply a filter hashtable or filter via a script block.
The instance name (dev.azure.com/username or your TFS host name)
Your collection. Defaults to DefaultCollection
The port of your installation/VSTS instance
The friendly name of the build step. Accepts wildcard characters
Indicates that SSL should be used
The VSTS access token as returned by Get-TfsAccessTokenString
Skip certificate validation

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> New-CATemplate -TemplateName WebServerCustom -DisplayName "Custom Web Server" -SourceTemplateName WebServer -RenewalPeriod 60.00:00:00

dev:remarks.maml:para: Duplicates the template WebServer with the new name WebServerCustom and a renewal period of 60 days

maml:description.maml:para: Create a new runspace pool
Create a new runspace pool with the specified throttle limit, apartment state, functions and variables
The throttle limit
The thread apartment state, e.g STA (single-threaded) or MTA (multi-threaded)
A list of variables transferred into each runspace pool. Shared by the entire pool
A list of functions transferred into each runspace pool. Shared by the entire pool
The thread apartment state, e.g STA (single-threaded) or MTA (multi-threaded)
The throttle limit
A list of variables transferred into each runspace pool. Shared by the entire pool
A list of functions transferred into each runspace pool. Shared by the entire pool

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a runspace pool with a maximum of 10 runspaces and transfers the variable Credential and Path to it.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> function eggs { 'eggs' }; function ham {'ham'}; New-RunspacePool -ThrottleLimit 5 -Function (Get-Command eggs,ham)

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a runspace pool with a maximum of 5 runspaces and imports the functions eggs and ham into its initial session state, ready for use.

command:example.dev:code:
$p = @() $p += (New-Object pscustomobject -Property @{ role = 'administrator'; identityDescriptor = 'System.Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity;S-1-5-21-3840877469-1399940413-2468247932-1000' }) $p += (New-Object pscustomobject -Property @{ role = 'administrator'; identityDescriptor = 'Microsoft.TeamFoundation.Identity;S-1-9-1551374245-688677674-3599067981-2526903658-827877225-0-0-0-0-1' }) $p += (New-Object pscustomobject -Property @{ role = 'administrator'; identityDescriptor = 'Microsoft.TeamFoundation.Identity;S-1-9-1551374245-3941645230-359989830-2867240742-1249722431-0-0-0-0-1' }) $p += (New-Object pscustomobject -Property @{ role = 'reader'; identityDescriptor = 'System.Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity;S-1-5-21-3840877469-1399940413-2468247932-513' })
New-TfsFeed @defaultParam -FeedName New2 Set-TfsFeedPermissions @defaultParam -FeedName New2 -Permissions $p

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new feed and assignes permissions. Makre sure the SIDs are taken from the domain the TFS / Azure DevOps server is a member of.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-TfsProject -InstanceName DSCTFS01 -Port 8080 -UseSsl -ProjectGuid ca8a6f46-66d7-4c9b-af99-61d97c9c407f -NewName TheOnlyConstantIsChange

dev:remarks.maml:para: Renames the project with the GUID ca8a6f46-66d7-4c9b-af99-61d97c9c407f to "TheOnlyConstantIsChange"

maml:description.maml:para: Synchronise cmdlet parameters with a hashtable
The CommandInfo or FunctionInfo object
The parameter hashtable
Indicates that the parameter values should be converted if possible. Throws an error if not possible.
The CommandInfo or FunctionInfo object
The parameter hashtable
Indicates that the parameter values should be converted if possible. Throws an error if not possible.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $parma = Sync-Parameter -Command (Get-Command New-Item) -Parameters $PSBoundParameters PS C:\> New-Item @parma

dev:remarks.maml:para: Grabs all valid parameters for New-Item from the PSBoundParameters collection in order to splat them to New-Item

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $parma = Sync-Parameter -Command (Get-Command New-Item) -Parameters $PSBoundParameters -ConvertValue PS C:\> New-Item @parma

dev:remarks.maml:para: Grabs all valid parameters for New-Item from the PSBoundParameters collection in order to splat them to New-Item, converting the parameter values as well

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $bla = @{UserName = 'Hans'; Password = 'Swordfish'; HostName = 'Invalid'} PS C:\> Test-Hashtable -Hashtable $bla -MandatoryKeys UserName, Password -ValidKeys UserName, Password, ComputerName

dev:remarks.maml:para: Writes an error since HostName is an invalid key

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $bla = @{UserName = 'Hans'; Password = 'Swordfish'; HostName = 'Invalid'} PS C:\> Test-Hashtable -Hashtable $bla -MandatoryKeys UserName, Password -ValidKeys UserName, Password, ComputerName -Quiet

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns false and does not write an error

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $bla = @{UserName = 'Hans';} PS C:\> Test-Hashtable -Hashtable $bla -MandatoryKeys UserName, Password -ValidKeys UserName, Password, ComputerName

dev:remarks.maml:para: Writes an error since Password is required
